message:
feat: pane chrome & focus clarity with title styling and split handle divider (2dccg.9.1)

Adds STYLE_PANE_TITLE_FOCUSED (accent+bold), STYLE_PANE_TITLE_UNFOCUSED (muted),
and STYLE_SPLIT_HANDLE tokens. Wires border_style() on Block for immediate focus
clarity. Renders split handle as │ vertical divider in dual-pane mode. 8 new tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","title":"Track D: Detail pane affordance polish (find bar, focus cues, readability)","description":"## Objective
Improve in-pane navigability and reduce visual ambiguity in the right-hand detail panel.

## Why This Exists
The detail find bar currently appears as flat text with no container styling, making active search state easy to miss and reducing confidence in keyboard-driven navigation.

## Scope
- Add visual treatment for find bar (container, contrast, focus signal)
- Ensure match counters/current match are legible across themes
- Verify no overlap with content scroll indicator and tab row

## Deliverables
- Find bar styling that is clearly distinct from content body
- Themed, accessible contrast levels for find query + match state
- Test coverage for render behavior when find bar is open/closed

## Completion Criteria
- Users can instantly distinguish content vs. find UI
- Find bar remains readable in all theme presets and degradation modes
- Existing find keybindings (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`) remain intact","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.295454800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.067444857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track D Intent
Find-in-detail is a high-frequency workflow. Visual ambiguity here disproportionately hurts UX, even if search core logic is correct.

### Quality Bar
The find bar should read like an interactive control, not ambient text. Active query and match counters must remain legible across themes and narrow layouts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","title":"Track E: Recalibrate degradation policy to preserve essential styling","description":"## Objective
Tune degradation behavior so terminals with sufficient capability keep meaningful styling rather than prematurely collapsing into bland output.

## Why This Exists
A suspected contributor to the quality drop is over-aggressive degradation logic tied to frame budget/terminal capability that strips colors/backgrounds too early.

## Scope
- Audit degradation decisions in render pipeline
- Define minimum visual hierarchy that should survive moderate degradation
- Adjust thresholds/policy and validate against representative terminal profiles

## Deliverables
- Documented degradation policy table (capability -> retained style layers)
- Updated heuristics/thresholds or guards in code
- Regression checks for no-style / low-style / full-style scenarios

## Completion Criteria
- Capable terminals retain tabs/pills/role hierarchy consistently
- Low-capability terminals remain readable without broken contrast
- Behavior is deterministic and testable (not ad-hoc per frame)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.544781336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.698611175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track E Intent
FrankenTUI’s degradation system is powerful, but if tuned too aggressively it can erase the very hierarchy we worked to build.

### Strategy
Audit first, then tune. Avoid blind threshold changes. Preserve readability and hierarchy while keeping low-capability terminals functional.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","title":"Audit degradation decision flow and identify style layers stripped per level","description":"## Goal
Make degradation behavior explicit and inspectable.

## Work Items

- Enumerate which style layers are disabled at each degradation stage
- Produce a compact matrix: degradation level -> retained UI affordances

## Deliverables

- Candidate list of over-aggressive strips affecting pills/tabs/gutters/markdown

## Acceptance
- Team can point to exact logic responsible for visual flattening
- Findings are specific enough to guide policy adjustments in follow-up tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179984071Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179961128Z","close_reason":"Completed degradation-flow audit with code-path mapping, per-level strip matrix, and follow-up candidate list documented in bead comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:56:39.253370420Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":358,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:26Z"},{"id":429,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit findings (2026-02-08) — degradation decision flow and stripped-style matrix

Code-path map
1. Runtime budget profile is configured in `src/ui/app.rs:12753-12760` using `FrameBudgetConfig { total: 120ms, allow_frame_skip: false, degradation_cooldown: 12, upgrade_threshold: 0.25, ..relaxed() }`.
2. Render-time degradation gates are read in `src/ui/app.rs:11615-11637` (`frame.degradation`) and projected into four booleans: `use_unicode_borders()`, `render_decorative()`, `apply_styling()`, `render_content()`.
3. Those gates directly control:
   - border type fallback (`Rounded` -> `Square`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11619-11623`
   - whether pane/modal borders render (`Borders::ALL` vs `Borders::NONE`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11626-11630`
   - whether semantic styles are applied vs plain monochrome at `src/ui/app.rs:11636-11647` and related style slots
   - whether search query inset styling is shown at `src/ui/app.rs:11804-11815`
   - whether pane content body is rendered (analytics/sources/modal content paths) at `src/ui/app.rs:12138-12358`
4. Degradation-level semantics come from ftui in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs:621-730`:
   - `Full`: unicode borders + styling + decorative + content
   - `SimpleBorders`: unicode off, styling/decor/content still on
   - `NoStyling`: unicode off, styling off, decor/content on
   - `EssentialOnly`: unicode off, styling off, decorative off, content on
   - `Skeleton`: content off (layout shells only)
   - `SkipFrame`: max degradation

Observed stripping matrix (current behavior)
- `Full`: no strip
- `SimpleBorders`: rounded/box-drawing affordance downgraded to square/ascii-like look
- `NoStyling`: all semantic color/attribute hierarchy collapses to plain style (chips/tabs/gutters cues flatten)
- `EssentialOnly`: borders/decorative chrome removed (`Borders::NONE`) while content remains
- `Skeleton`: content text suppressed in gated paths; pane shells persist
- `SkipFrame`: max level; in this app frame-skip is disabled (`allow_frame_skip=false`), but visual output remains at effectively skeleton/no-content behavior where gates apply

Cross-check with tests
- Enum progression + gate semantics validated in `src/ui/app.rs:16452-16479`.
- Border suppression expectation validated in `src/ui/app.rs:17129-17152`.

Candidate over-aggressive flattening contributors for follow-up (`2dccg.5.2` / `2dccg.10.6`)
- Hard style collapse at `NoStyling` removes semantic differentiation too early for capable terminals under transient pressure.
- `render_decorative()` gate currently disables all borders at `EssentialOnly`, which can reduce scannability in dense panes.
- Decision thresholds for controller/e-process are largely inherited from ftui defaults (`ftui-render/src/budget.rs:342-357`, `454-481`) and should be revalidated against cass workload characteristics.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","title":"Tune degradation thresholds and smooth transitions for capable terminals","description":"## Goal
Rebalance degradation so essential hierarchy survives on capable terminals, and avoid abrupt visual cliffs when degradation level changes at runtime.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **runtime degradation transition dynamics**.
- Includes: threshold tuning, hysteresis/debouncing, and smooth level-to-level transition behavior.
- Excludes: capability-to-feature policy mapping for decorative/border strategy (`2dccg.10.6`), which consumes these tuned transition dynamics.

## Phase 1: Threshold Tuning (original 2dccg.5.2)
- Review current degradation trigger points (frame time, memory, resize frequency).
- Identify which style layers are most impactful for readability vs purely decorative.
- Raise thresholds where terminals can handle richer rendering without instability.
- Validate that tuned thresholds don't cause flickering or oscillation.

## Phase 2: Smooth Transitions (absorbed from 2dccg.10.8)

What changed
- Added a dedicated cass runtime budget profile helper: `cass_runtime_budget_config()` in `src/ui/app.rs`.
- Switched runtime startup to consume that helper in `run_tui_ftui()` instead of ad-hoc inline values.

New profile values
- `total = 120ms` (unchanged high-level quality-first target)
- `phase_budgets = { diff: 6ms, present: 12ms, render: 24ms }` (explicitly scaled relative to default 2/4/8)
- `allow_frame_skip = false` (unchanged)
- `degradation_cooldown = 20` (up from 12)
- `upgrade_threshold = 0.40` (up from 0.25)

Why this addresses 5.2
- Higher cooldown + higher upgrade threshold reduce level-flapping under transient pressure.
- Explicit phase budgets make overrun signals less opaque and align better with the 120ms frame target.
- Frame-skip remains disabled to avoid abrupt visual disappearance.

Tests added
1. `cass_runtime_budget_profile_is_quality_first_and_stable`
   - Asserts the full profile contract above.
2. `cass_runtime_budget_upgrade_is_debounced_by_cooldown`
   - Asserts upgrades are blocked until cooldown frames elapse, then allowed under strong headroom.

Validation run
- `cargo fmt --check` (pass)
- `cargo check --all-targets` (pass)
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` (pass)
- `cargo test cass_runtime_budget_profile_is_quality_first_and_stable -- --nocapture` (pass)

Note on targeted second test execution
- `cargo test cass_runtime_budget_upgrade_is_debounced_by_cooldown -- --nocapture` currently collides with pre-existing unrelated `src/ui/style_system.rs` unit-test import errors (`super::app::LayoutBreakpoint`) in this shared branch context; those errors are outside this bead’s changed codepath.

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","title":"Add degradation and capability-matrix regression tests with diagnostic fixtures","description":"## Goal

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)

- Verify that information hierarchy (headers > body > metadata) survives degradation.
- Assert specific style properties preserved/stripped at each level boundary.
- Test interaction behavior (keyboard nav, mouse clicks) is unaffected by degradation.

## Phase 2: Capability-Matrix Diagnostics (absorbed from 2dccg.10.9)
- Define capability matrix: color profile x Unicode support x frame budget x terminal type.

- Create test fixtures for common terminal profiles (xterm-256color, screen, dumb, Windows Terminal, kitty).
- Add tests that verify style-policy decisions match documented intent for each profile.
- Provide machine-readable diagnostic output for debugging unexpected visual behavior.
- Validate that NO_COLOR, CASS_RESPECT_NO_COLOR, and TERM override behavior is consistent.

- A regression that over-strips styles at any degradation level is caught by tests.
- Each capability profile has documented expected behavior and tested invariants.

- Create richer fixture data (multi-agent roles, tool calls, markdown/code blocks, long snippets, score spread)
- Add snapshot variants covering pills, tabs, detail view, and role gutters
- Ensure tests are deterministic and cheap enough for regular local/CI runs

## Deliverables
- Expanded fixture builders/data helpers
- New snapshot baselines for key visual surfaces
- Test docs clarifying how to regenerate snapshots when intentional UI changes happen

## Completion Criteria
- Visual regressions in key surfaces produce obvious snapshot diffs
- Fixtures represent realistic cass workloads (not toy text-only lines)
- Snapshot updates are deliberate and reviewable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.796092040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.594645265Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track F Intent

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","title":"Add search-surface regression tests (structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy)","description":"## Goal
Protect search-surface rendering with tests that verify structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy remain correct after polish changes.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead is the **single owner** of search-surface regression coverage.
- Includes: search bar, filter pills, breadcrumbs, footer HUD, and search-input affordance behavior.
- Excludes: results-surface regression ownership (`2dccg.9.5`), global interaction state-machine routing ownership (`2dccg.11.2`), cross-theme/degradation matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`), and full workflow E2E playback (`2dccg.11.3`).
- Must reuse baseline fixtures/helpers from `2dccg.6.2` instead of duplicating baseline snapshot setup.

## Test Scenarios

### Structure Tests
- Search bar renders with mode-aware title and cursor position.
- Filter pills render with correct style tokens (STYLE_PILL_ACTIVE for active, standard for inactive).
- Breadcrumb trail renders with clickable segments and correct emphasis.
- Footer HUD renders with KBD key/desc token styling.
- Layout respects LayoutBreakpoint constraints (search bar width, pill wrapping).

### Interaction Tests
- Tab key cycles through filter pills in expected order.
- Enter key on search bar triggers search.
- Click on pill toggles active/inactive state.
- Escape key clears search input or exits mode.
- Mouse hover on interactive elements shows correct cursor affordance.

### Visual Hierarchy Tests
- Active filter pills have higher visual weight than inactive.
- Search bar input area is visually distinct from surrounding chrome.
- Footer key hints maintain KBD/desc pairing across themes.
- Theme switch updates all search-surface styles immediately.

## Logging
- Each test case logs: test_id, component, action, expected_state, actual_state.
- Failures include terminal size, active theme, degradation level.
- Use structured logging from `2dccg.11.6` when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All search-surface regressions from the ftui migration are caught.
- Tests run in < 3s total and require no terminal (headless).
- Test names clearly indicate which search-surface element is being validated.
- Suite composes with `2dccg.6.2`/`2dccg.6.3`/`2dccg.11.x` without duplicate ownership.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:32.127079571Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:16.298074887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:36.523677893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:40.892002824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:46.311618406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:52:05.096187123Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
